title: Source lineage for The Wars of Alexander: Skeat's 1886 edited selection
summary: The General Corpus artifact is a pinned copy of U-M encoding APA1967.0001.001, which encodes selected matter from Walter W. Skeat's 1886 edition. That edition contains two distinct historical works: Wars of Alexander (Fragment C), printed through overlapping Ashmole 44 and TCD MS 213 witness streams, and a separate prose Story of Alexander from TCD MS 213. The U-M raw package, its encoding payload, its 506-TIFF printed-volume facsimile, the Kraus unaltered reprint, and the Warburg PDF facsimile are modeled as distinct layers.
review_notes: Primary transmission path — Wars via Ashmole: artifact:general-corpus:apa1967 -> encoding:umich:APA1967.0001.001 -> edition:skeat:wars-of-alexander:1886 -> witness:bodleian:ashmole-44. Ashmole is the larger base stream; its lacuna is supplied from Dublin.

Primary transmission path — Wars via Dublin: artifact:general-corpus:apa1967 -> encoding:umich:APA1967.0001.001 -> edition:skeat:wars-of-alexander:1886 -> witness:tcd:ms-213. This overlaps Ashmole and is not an additive second half.

Primary transmission path — Story via Dublin: artifact:general-corpus:apa1967 -> encoding:umich:APA1967.0001.001 -> edition:skeat:wars-of-alexander:1886 -> witness:tcd:ms-213. The prose is a separate work at current ff. 70v-72r after intervening account leaves.
Supporting relationship — The U-M raw package supplies both the raw encoding payload and the 506-TIFF printed-edition facsimile payload.
Supporting relationship — The Digital Bodleian object supplies one image of Ashmole 44 fol. 1r only.
Supporting relationship — The Historia de Preliis J3a relation identifies a source tradition, not a direct codex-to-codex transmission claim.
Supporting relationship — The proposed Caxton relation is supported/medium and remains distinct from confirmed primary paths.
Supporting relationship — The Warburg PDF is a facsimile of a Kraus unaltered reprint copied from Skeat 1886; it is not the physical 1886 U-M scan object.
Supporting relationship — Stevenson 1849 and Duggan-Turville-Petre 1989 are supporting editions, not ancestors of apa1967.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for apa1967
entities.description: The exact pinned XML artifact consumed by the repository. After removal of generated node and note identifiers plus the processing-profile value, its canonicalized structure and text are byte-identical to the raw U-M XML.

entities.label: U-M raw distribution package for APA1967.0001.001
entities.description: The complete provider ZIP distributed through Internet Archive: 511 members comprising 506 TIFF page images and five raw encoding, OCR, header, page-order, and processing-support files. The package contains but is not identical to either the encoding or TIFF facsimile layer.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME encoding APA1967.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 TEI Libraries Level 1 encoding of selected matter from Skeat's 1886 edition. The raw provider XML reports no corrections or editing. It retains title matter, the verse edition, Skeat's ending paraphrase, The Story of Alexander, notes, and page-image links while omitting most front and back text.

entities.label: U-M 506-TIFF scan layer for Skeat's 1886 volume
entities.description: The complete 506-image sequence in the U-M raw package. It includes the full provider-published scan object for the printed volume, including matter omitted from the text encoding. Completeness is relative to the provider object, not proof that every binding surface was photographed.

entities.label: Walter W. Skeat, The Wars of Alexander (EETS Extra Series 47, 1886)
entities.description: The immediate printed source of the U-M encoding. Skeat prints MS Ashmole 44 and TCD MS 213 on facing pages where they overlap, supplies the Ashmole lacuna from Dublin, paraphrases the lost ending editorially, and appends a separate incomplete prose Story of Alexander from the Dublin codex.

bibliographic.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., The Wars of Alexander: An Alliterative Romance Translated Chiefly from the Historia Alexandri Magni de Preliis, EETS Extra Series 47 (London: N. Trübner, 1886).
bibliographic.title: The Wars of Alexander
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Extra Series 47

entities.label: Kraus unaltered reprint of Skeat's 1886 Wars of Alexander
entities.description: An undated Kraus Reprint Co. physical reprint whose preliminary statement calls it an unaltered reprint produced with EETS permission. It reproduces the 1886 title page and text but is a distinct print carrier. Because the schema has no general print-carrier type and this is not a new scholarly editing act, catalog_description is the deliberate workaround.

bibliographic.citation: Kraus Reprint Co., unaltered reprint of Walter W. Skeat, The Wars of Alexander, EETS Extra Series 47 (1886), undated.

entities.label: Warburg PDF scan of the complete Kraus reprint of Skeat 1886
entities.description: A complete 512-page provider PDF. The original 1886 title page is PDF page 9, the edition text begins at PDF page 31, and the target selection ends at PDF page 313. It is a scan of the Kraus reprint, not the same physical 1886 copy represented by U-M's TIFF package.
entities.reproduction_format: 512-page PDF page-image scan with OCR text layer

entities.label: Wars of Alexander (Alexander Fragment C), abstract work
entities.description: The anonymous Middle English unrhymed alliterative Alexander romance, distinct from Alexander Fragments A and B and from the prose Story appended by Skeat. The schema has no abstract-work entity type, so catalog_description is used with this explicit note.

date_statements.label: Composition
date_statements.notes: Do not conflate this work-level estimate with witness copying dates.

entities.label: The Story of Alexander, abstract prose work
entities.description: A separate incomplete Middle English prose epitome preserved at TCD MS 213 ff. 70v-72r and editorially titled by Skeat. The proposed relation to Caxton's second Dicts and Sayings edition remains supported rather than confirmed. The schema has no abstract-work entity type, so catalog_description is used.

date_statements.label: First known attestation
date_statements.notes: This is witness attestation, not a secure composition range.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Ashmole 44, ff. 1r-97v
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century northern English paper codex containing the longest and most complete witness of Wars of Alexander as its single text. The work is imperfect at the end and has a lacuna between current fols. 12 and 13.

notes: One column, 25-34 lines; one scribe in Anglicana.
Catalog date 1440-1460; substantially Durham or possibly south Northumberland dialect with north-west Midland features.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, IE TCD MS 213 (formerly D.4.12)
entities.description: A composite fifteenth-century paper codex. Wars of Alexander occupies current ff. 27r-66v; accounts and miscellanea occupy ff. 67r-70r; the separate prose Story occupies ff. 70v-72r. Skeat's restarted foliation calls the prose leaves 45v-47r.

date_statements.label: Manuscript production
date_statements.notes: Retain the broad range pending a current item-level TCD catalog record.

holding.notes: No stable current item-level catalog URL was verified as of 2026-07-17.

physical_description.extent: Composite codex; target verse ff. 27r-66v and target prose ff. 70v-72r

entities.label: Digital Bodleian one-image surrogate of MS Ashmole 44
entities.description: A one-canvas public object showing only fol. 1r, the opening page of Wars of Alexander. It is partial to the codex and must never be described as a complete manuscript facsimile.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation object with one color JPEG from a 35mm slide

entities.label: Historia de Preliis, recension J3a, source tradition
entities.description: The Latin Alexander source tradition identified for the Middle English verse. This is a textual-source relation, not a claim that either surviving English codex was copied directly from a particular identified Latin manuscript.

entities.label: William Caxton's second edition of The Dicts and Sayings of the Philosophers, circa 1480
entities.description: Proposed printed source for the prose Story. The schema has no early-print class, so catalog_description is the deliberate print-carrier workaround; it must not be mislabeled as a scholarly editing act. The relation remains supported/medium pending direct collation and exact edition identification.

entities.label: Joseph Stevenson, The Alliterative Romance of Alexander (1849)
entities.description: An earlier edition of the Ashmole witness, useful as supporting edition history but not the immediate source of the U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Joseph Stevenson, ed., The Alliterative Romance of Alexander (Roxburghe Club, 1849).
bibliographic.title: The Alliterative Romance of Alexander

entities.label: Hoyt N. Duggan and Thorlac Turville-Petre, The Wars of Alexander (EETS Supplementary Series 10, 1989)
entities.description: A modern scholarly edition and supporting access lead, not an ancestor of the U-M encoding. Its exact witness treatment was not directly verified, so no transcribes relation is projected from the catalog record alone.

bibliographic.citation: Hoyt N. Duggan and Thorlac Turville-Petre, eds., The Wars of Alexander, EETS Supplementary Series 10 (Oxford University Press, 1989).
bibliographic.title: The Wars of Alexander
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Supplementary Series 10

access.format: Catalog record; library or purchase access to the edition

rights.component: Exact pinned General Corpus/CME XML artifact
rights.jurisdiction: United States; provider and repository terms
rights.basis: U-M currently labels the hosted materials public domain/No Copyright - United States, while the inherited raw header retains a conditional copyright notice and the CME Unlicense expressly speaks of software. No broader artifact license is inferred across text, data, and markup layers.
notes: The separate provider and repository statements are preserved rather than collapsed into one license conclusion.

rights.component: Raw distribution package as delivered, including images, XML, OCR, header, and support files
rights.basis: The embedded legacy header says use is subject to copyright where applicable and limits copying to scholarship and research unless permission is obtained; the Internet Archive item supplies no verified item-level license overriding that package statement.
notes: This historical provider layer is distinct from the current U-M public-domain display.

rights.component: Current U-M hosted digital text and encoding
rights.basis: The current U-M browser states that the materials are in the public domain and displays the RightsStatements.org No Copyright - United States designation.
notes: Retain alongside the older raw-header statement; this record does not erase the historical distribution layer.

rights.component: Current U-M page-image presentation and the provider TIFF scan layer
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies its public-domain/No Copyright - United States statement to the hosted materials. No separate item-level license for the historical raw ZIP was verified.
Published in 1886 and therefore in the public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Undated Kraus reprint carrier and any carrier-specific presentation
rights.basis: The reprint is undated and no item-level license or evidence establishing the legal status of carrier-specific presentation was verified, even though its underlying 1886 editorial text is public domain in the United States.

rights.component: Warburg provider PDF scan and OCR layer
rights.basis: The PDF is publicly downloadable, but no item-level open license for the scan or OCR layer was verified. Public access alone is not reuse permission.
The anonymous medieval work predates modern copyright protection.

rights.component: Underlying medieval Middle English prose work
rights.basis: The anonymous medieval work is attested in a late-fifteenth-century witness and predates modern copyright protection.

rights.component: Underlying medieval manuscript text and historical codex contents
rights.basis: The fifteenth-century manuscript text predates modern copyright protection; this does not determine rights or contract terms in modern reproductions.

rights.component: Any future Bodleian ordered images of MS Ashmole 44
rights.basis: Imaging-service terms and publication categories apply; commercial use requires permission and may incur fees. No terms for a future whole-codex delivery have been granted.
notes: Ask specifically about public Git redistribution, preservation/reference use, and a repository supporting commercial as well as noncommercial print-on-demand.

rights.component: Underlying medieval manuscript texts and historical codex contents
rights.basis: The fifteenth-century manuscript texts predate modern copyright protection; this does not determine rights in future digital surrogates.

rights.component: Any future TCD ordered images of IE TCD MS 213
rights.jurisdiction: Ireland; TCD service and licensing terms
rights.basis: TCD's 2025 policy makes CC BY 4.0 the default for qualifying out-of-copyright Library-created surrogates absent conflicts, but no item-level license for MS 213 or future order files was verified.
notes: Obtain written item-specific permission before redistribution, especially in a Git repository supporting commercial print-on-demand.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian image of MS Ashmole 44 fol. 1r
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest attributes the image Photo © Bodleian Libraries, University of Oxford and applies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: The noncommercial condition does not authorize commercial or for-profit print-on-demand reuse.

rights.basis: The medieval source tradition predates modern copyright protection; modern editions and facsimiles remain separate layers.

rights.component: Underlying circa-1480 printed text and historical print carrier
rights.basis: The fifteenth-century printed text and carrier predate modern copyright protection; this record does not apply to any modern scan.
Published in 1849 and therefore in the public domain in the United States.
Published in 1989; no item-level open license was verified. The Folger catalog record provides discovery, not permission to reproduce the edition.

editorial_practices.representation: Selected title matter and printed pp. 1-283 are transcribed; omitted front and back text remains represented chiefly by PB links.
editorial_practices.description: The U-M encoding is an edited selection from the printed volume, not a full-text transcription of all 506 scanned pages.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole encoding and its front/back omission boundary

editorial_practices.source_feature: Overlapping Ashmole 44 and TCD MS 213 verse witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Facing or parallel witness streams where they overlap, with Dublin also supplying the Ashmole lacuna.
editorial_practices.description: The two verse streams are alternative witnesses of one work and are non-additive; serialized line-element counts cannot be summed as poem length.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete verse ending and separate Dublin prose work
editorial_practices.representation: Skeat supplies an editorial prose paraphrase through p. 279, then an argument and separately headed prose Story on pp. 279-283.
editorial_practices.description: The ending paraphrase is editorial rather than medieval verse, and The Story of Alexander is a distinct historical work rather than the final prose section of the verse romance.

editorial_practices.representation: The XML serializes 5,688 Ashmole-stream L elements and 2,821 Dublin-stream L elements across 40 verse-group containers, with page milestones preserving the printed order.
editorial_practices.description: Encoding order and line-element counts describe the digital representation, not an intellectual 8,509-line poem.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., The Wars of Alexander, EETS Extra Series 47 (London: N. Trübner, 1886).

evidence.citation: Warburg Institute Library digital PDF of the Kraus unaltered reprint of Skeat's Wars of Alexander.

evidence.citation: Joseph Stevenson, ed., The Alliterative Romance of Alexander (Roxburghe Club, 1849).
evidence.summary: Supporting evidence for the earlier Ashmole-only edition identified and criticized by Skeat; it is not an ancestor of apa1967.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Wars of Alexander (Alexander Fragment C).

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium, bibliography BIB3575, Wars of Alexander.
evidence.summary: Confirms NIMEV *3947.3, DIMEV 6306, both witnesses, Skeat EETS ES 47, and the preferred-manuscript/Dublin-lacuna arrangement.

open_questions.question: Can Skeat's conventional 5,677 endpoint, the XML's 5,688 Ashmole L nodes, and the Bodleian catalog's 5,803/approximately-6,300 account be crosswalked without conflating editorial systems?
next_steps: Collate the systems against passus, witness, and lacuna boundaries before supplying an intellectual line extent.

open_questions.question: Can Skeat's Dublin line ranges be reconciled with the 2024 Bodleian catalog's modern ranges?
next_steps: Create a page-and-line concordance using a complete Dublin reproduction and the modern edition.

open_questions.question: What is the current stable item-level Trinity catalog URL and complete physical description for IE TCD MS 213?
next_steps: Ask Trinity Research Collections for the current catalog record and shelfmark history.

open_questions.question: Does direct collation confirm that The Story of Alexander derives from Caxton's second Dicts and Sayings edition, and which exact printing is intended?
next_steps: Collate TCD ff. 70v-72r against the second-edition print witness and Blake 1991.

open_questions.question: Will Bodleian provide a complete-color or complete-reference facsimile of MS Ashmole 44, and under what repository/publication license and quote?
next_steps: Request a whole-codex quote and written redistribution terms; identify target ff. 1r-97v.

open_questions.question: Will TCD provide a complete-color facsimile of IE TCD MS 213, and does an item-specific CC BY 4.0 statement apply to delivered files?
next_steps: Request the complete codex, identify verse ff. 27r-66v and prose ff. 70v-72r, and obtain item-specific reuse terms.

open_questions.question: What exact preferred authority identity should replace Skeat's source-limited Mr. Hessels, and can George Parker be normalized beyond Skeat's printed name?
next_steps: Search authority files and EETS correspondence without expanding either display name from inference alone.
